incident.symptom: Top-10 replay tasks failed with KeyError: 'symbol' while loading partitioned stock-bar parquet data.
incident.root_cause: The research replay loader first lost key=value partition columns from parquet paths. After that was fixed, single-symbol GCS download shards still exposed stock-bar frames without a stable symbol column. The final replay blocker was the embedded Batch GCS helper stripping the trailing slash from directory prefixes; relative object paths began with '/', so downloads were written outside the intended input tree and replays ran against empty local inputs.
incident.operational_effect: No trading, broker-facing, live-manifest, or risk-policy effect. The failed replay output is diagnosis-only.

commits.change: Restore parquet partition columns in option replay.

runner_fix.validation: Targeted runner tests passed: 11 passed across option replay loader, option-aware replay, fill ladder dataset, and fill ladder aggregation tests.

embedded_batch_helper_fix.change: Preserve trailing slash in split_uri by using lstrip instead of strip, and defensively lstrip the computed relative object path before joining under the destination input directory.
embedded_batch_helper_fix.invalidated_reason: Replay completed against empty local stock/option inputs and all candidates showed no_source_stock_trades.

parallel_lane.next_step_after_success: Aggregate fill coverage and launch next-10 replay only for symbols whose 365d_5x5 dataset passes the 0.90 fill gate.

guardrails: Do not trade from raw research output.
Do not arm an execution window from this packet.
Do not start broker-facing paper or live sessions from this packet.
Do not modify live manifests, strategy selection, or risk policy from this packet.
Do not relax the 0.90 fill gate for promotion review.
Tomorrow's paper-session use requires a separate operator/control-plane decision after promotion-review packets are clean.
next_safe_step: Monitor the GCS-helper-fixed top-10 and next-10 replay jobs. If either succeeds, aggregate portfolio reports into promotion-review packets and continue until five or more research-governed candidates are available or a new blocker is proven.
